title: Yellow Vests, Pessimistic Beliefs, and Carbon Tax Aversion

tax_mentions.context: Respondents are asked to approve/disapprove a carbon tax increase whose revenues are redistributed uniformly to each adult.
tax_mentions.notes: Uses “Would you approve of this reform?”; measures acceptance/approval.

tax_mentions.context: Carbon-tax reform with dividend paid only to adults below income thresholds; used to estimate causal impact of “winning” beliefs on acceptance.
Random housing- or transport-sector excise-tax increase financing a lump-sum transfer; elicits perceived win/lose and elasticities.
tax_mentions.notes: Primarily belief elicitation; approval analyzed for the full tax & dividend.

excluded_policy_mentions.reason_it_is_not_a_carbon_or_fuel_tax: EU ETS is a permit-trading scheme (emissions trading); not a carbon/fuel excise tax.

inclusion_reason: Directly measures French public approval/opposition to a carbon tax with dividend (uniform and targeted) and analyzes determinants via survey experiments/information and belief-based causal effects.
